Elena Kowalska
Ul. Mokotowska 52 m. 7, 00-543 Warszawa, Poland
+48 511 380 224
elena.kowalska@protonmail.com
linkedin.com/in/elenakowalska
Personal Profile
Project coordinator with seven years of EU-funded research programme administration,
known for keeping fourteen-partner consortia on schedule and under budget. Fluent in
Polish, English and German, and comfortable anywhere between a ministry meeting in
Brussels and a field station in Svalbard.
Work Experience
May 2019 - Present
Project Coordinator
Institute of Geophysics, Polish Academy of Sciences, Warszawa
- Coordinated three Horizon 2020 consortia totalling 14 partner institutions and a 6.2 million EUR budget
- Prepared periodic technical and financial reports, all accepted by the European Commission without revision
- Organised 11 general assemblies and review meetings in 6 countries, hosting up to 90 participants
- Negotiated two grant agreement amendments, recovering 240,000 EUR of unspent funds for the consortium
Sep 2014 - Apr 2019
Research Administrator
University of Warsaw, Faculty of Physics
- Administered 23 national grants from the National Science Centre (NCN) worth 18 million PLN in total
- Built the faculty's first grant-deadline tracking database in Access, later migrated to SharePoint
- Drafted budgets and eligibility checks for more than 60 proposals per year, raising the success rate to 31%
Education and Training
Oct 2012 - Jun 2014
Master of Arts in International Relations, University of Warsaw, thesis on EU research policy
Oct 2008 - Jun 2012
Bachelor of Arts in European Studies, Jagiellonian University, Kraków
Personal Skills
Mother tongue
Polish
Other languages
English - C2 (IELTS 8.5), German - B2 (Goethe-Zertifikat), Russian - B1
Communication skills
Confident public speaker; chaired plenary sessions of up to 90 delegates and presented
results at two European Geosciences Union meetings in Vienna.
Organisational and managerial skills
Led a distributed support team of 12 administrators across 6 institutions; introduced a
shared risk register that cut reporting delays by half.
Computer skills
MS Project, Excel with pivot tables and Power Query, SharePoint, Power BI, Zotero, basic SQL
Driving licence
Category B
